svg.text: ← written by memory write node · read by context hydration node

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is stored in short-term conversation memory?')
g.text: Tier 1 · short-term context
Last 7 days of full conversation turns · Supabase messages table · injected as sliding window into every agent system prompt
Supabase Realtime subscription keeps client in sync during multi-turn flows

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does pgvector semantic memory retrieval work?')
g.text: Tier 2 · long-term semantic memory · Mem0 / Honcho
Key facts extracted per turn stored as embeddings in Supabase pgvector
Cosine similarity retrieval at session start · top-k facts injected into context
e.g. "user has T2 diabetes" persists across months · immune to 7d window

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the difference between static and dynamic user profile facts?')
g.text: Tier 3 · user profile store · Supabase + Redis cache
Static facts
DOB · sex · height · allergies
diagnosed conditions · medications
set once · rarely change
Dynamic facts
Current goals · avg daily calories (7d)
last workout · recent mood / energy
updated per turn by fact extractor

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How are constraint rules auto-derived from the user profile?')
g.text: Auto-derived constraint rules
Profile facts → rule engine → constraint set prepended to every agent system prompt
Examples: T2 diabetes → no refined sugar · metformin → no alcohol · nut allergy → no tree nuts
Rules validated again post-synthesis by constraint validator node

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Redis cache the user profile and what is the TTL strategy?')
g.text: Redis profile cache + LangGraph checkpointer
User profile cached with 5-min TTL · invalidated on write · LangGraph state per session key
Rate limit counters · idempotency key store

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What are the Supabase RLS policies for health data?')
g.text: Supabase data layer · security
Row Level Security on all PHI tables · users see only their own data
pgvector for semantic embeddings · Realtime for client push · Storage for uploads
→ infrastructure & observability (diagram 4)
